things.name: Scone Palace
things.description: Historic seat of Scottish kings where monarchs were crowned on the famous Stone of Destiny — magnificent state rooms and fine art collection

things.name: Perth Museum
things.description: Newly redeveloped world-class museum in the heart of Perth housing the Stone of Destiny and the city's rich cultural heritage

things.name: Kinnoull Hill & Tower
things.description: Dramatic wooded hill above the Tay gorge with a folly tower and panoramic views over Perth and the surrounding countryside

things.name: Balhousie Castle (Black Watch Museum)
things.description: Headquarters museum of the Black Watch regiment with unique collections of medals, uniforms and Jacobite relics

things.name: Branklyn Garden (NTS)
things.description: Small but perfectly formed NTS garden with a remarkable collection of Himalayan alpine plants and rhododendrons

things.name: Elcho Castle
things.description: Well-preserved 16th-century tower house on the banks of the Tay with rare original wrought-iron window grilles

things.name: Huntingtower Castle
things.description: Medieval tower house with Scotland's finest surviving painted ceiling, scene of the Raid of Ruthven

things.name: Caithness Glass Visitor Centre
things.description: Famous Scottish glassmaking factory with free factory tours where you can watch molten glass transformed into beautiful objects

things.name: Perth Concert Hall
things.description: Award-winning concert hall in the city centre hosting a year-round programme of classical music, comedy and festivals

things.name: Loch Leven & Mary Queen of Scots Escape (Day Trip)
things.description: Historic loch island castle where Mary Queen of Scots was imprisoned and dramatically escaped — now an RSPB nature reserve
